metadata.description: Curated tutorial clues demonstrating each cryptic crossword wordplay type. Selected for clarity, common answers, and high confidence explanations.

anagram.description: Letters of one or more words are rearranged to form the answer. Look for an indicator like "broken", "mixed", or "scrambled" near the fodder words.

clues.clue_text: Guess teatimes varied
I'd augment bust size
Horrible Gaultier tie
Heats pan to cook game bird
Their woes compounded if not
Lops head off flowering plant
Create concoction of green tea
Dynamic change, hot I gathered
Fuel from isle Oscar lied about
Has Cnut ordered halt mid-flow?
Crackers seem moist on occasion
Ban lace pants to create stability
Hires out a buggy producing permit
Tender wound initially moist hardens
Decree nisi without one moving house
clues.ai_explanation: anagram of DECREENIS(Decree nisi without one)

clues.clue_text: Picked up perfumes for a little cash
Joint below or bowel given treatment
clues.ai_explanation: Anagram (given treatment) of below or bowel

clues.clue_text: One miracle involved splendid ritual
Smashing atoms up is creating element
Remedy sipped, say, getting indigestion
Reconstruct flue with us? That'll be handy
Soap star needs to work on posture finally
Terribly poor signs seeing medical forecast
Catches slowcoach delaying son’s appearance
clues.ai_explanation: (s)NAIL = slowcoach, delaying son's appearance is a deletion indicator

clues.clue_text: Maoists argued furiously blocking road south

charade.description: The answer is built by joining smaller pieces end to end, like links in a chain. Each piece of the clue gives you one chunk of the answer.

clues.clue_text: A free BBC
Minute mark
Doctor on boat in capital
Signified average temperature
City hospital to decline work
clues.ai_explanation: SAN (synonym of "hospital") + DIE (synonym of "decline") + GO (synonym of "work"); definition: "City"

components.sig_explanation: SAN (synonym of "hospital") + DIE (synonym of "decline") + GO (synonym of "work"); definition: "City"

clues.clue_text: Producing clubs with gold pipe
Fools lease land west of London
clues.ai_explanation: BERKS (synonym of "Fools") + HIRE (synonym of "lease"); definition: "land west of London"

components.sig_explanation: BERKS (synonym of "Fools") + HIRE (synonym of "lease"); definition: "land west of London"

clues.clue_text: Silly Queen cuts time with power
Glib talk close to fashion model
Discuss business while gobbling?
Dance with game business graduate
Detectives wait on southern swells
Fighter with degrees in Old Persian
Pure Charlie? It’s superior to speed
Soiled article brought to pawnbroker
Snack, one eaten by uncommon baronet
clues.ai_explanation: RARE(synonym of "uncommon") + B(first letter of "by") + I(abbr. "one") + T(last letter of "baronet")

clues.clue_text: Singer about to plug very old record
Former partner, full-figured specimen
Aida perhaps got naked for each adult
Check address of page, an old peasant
clues.ai_explanation: CH(abbr. "Check") + URL(address of page); definition: "an old peasant"

clues.clue_text: Silly play’s opening provided by cast
clues.ai_explanation: P(first letter of "play") + IF(abbr. "provided") + FLING(synonym of "cast"); definition: "Silly"

clues.clue_text: Rubbish as well, Scottish entertainer?
Press yellow base metal made from this?
Look old taking book inside summerhouse
Grand pound shop's opening showing pants
Refrain from church when finally contrite
Friend having top that's rather washed out
Heartless Conservative left to admit regret
Here peers sit, in Lords, returned to power
clues.ai_explanation: Peers (people who pee) in lords = CHAMBER reutorned (reverves) TO P(ower) = POT

clues.clue_text: Palace attendant’s first opening after noon
clues.ai_explanation: LATER(synonym of "after") + A(first letter of "attendant's") + N(first letter of "noon"); definition: "Palace attendant's"

container.description: One word or abbreviation is placed inside another to form the answer. Indicators like "in", "around", "holding", or "containing" signal this type.

clues.clue_text: Blue fish catching cold
Rebel force in ancient city
Hand is good, holding spades
Intend to wear ripped clothing
Wrong about second name of man
Quietly cutting gentleman's tree
Lake bed holds solidified matter
clues.ai_explanation: COT(synonym of "bed") + L(first letter of "Lake") [container]; definition: "solidified matter"

clues.clue_text: Old man covers chest in warm coat
Fellow from hotel wearing headgear
Single partner inside also excited
French city soon collects six grand
Ex-PM of Germany invested in real gold
Help comprehending case for philosopher
Popular Liberal upset butcher in garden
clues.ai_explanation: L = liberal = LLIK (kill (butcher) reversed (upset) contained by (in) WEED (garden

clues.clue_text: Start to tuck into sandwich and roly-poly
Slender holy object inscribed with C and E
Most inscrutable spy’s heading under cover?
clues.ai_explanation: BLANKET(cover) + S(first letter of "spy") [container]; definition: "Most inscrutable"

clues.clue_text: Italian banker fences recently stolen picture
clues.ai_explanation: HOT(synonym of "recently stolen") inside PO(synonym of "Italian banker" = River)

clues.clue_text: Cup of tea inspiring family activity in kitchen
Differences in Nicaraguan rebels crossing street
Loner’s suitable moment to return, before crowds
Drained theatre nurse maybe collars food supplier
clues.ai_explanation: TE(THEATRE minus HEATR, "theatre") + CARER(synonym of "nurse") [container]; definition: "food supplier"

clues.clue_text: Concern of PR department that is saving publication
Genuine article relative keeps, one cherished originally
Clergyman put back in prison in the US – a deep, dark hole
clues.ai_explanation: reverse REV(Clergyman), then VER inside CAN(prison)

clues.clue_text: Mother of pearly king dropped into ocean travelling with us
clues.ai_explanation: OCEAN(ocean) + US(us) + R(abbr. "king") [container]; definition: "Mother of pearly king"

clues.clue_text: Writer receiving consent for colour of boat in nonsense poem
Exclude nothing about sweetheart for party
Offer to put in fuel when up for drive
clues.ai_explanation: reverse OIL(fuel), then BID(offer) inside LIO

clues.clue_text: Person engaging lawyer Neil is back in court
clues.ai_explanation: LIEN(reversal of "Neil is back") inside CT(abbr. "court")

deletion.description: A letter or letters are removed from a word to give the answer. Watch for indicators like "losing", "without", "dropping", or "headless".

clues.clue_text: Spots litter leader’s left
Nobody is forgetting an old date
clues.ai_explanation: NONES(deletion from "Nobody") + (deletion from "old") [deletion]; definition: "an old date"

clues.clue_text: Be confronted by heartless travesty
Run out of fortress, free in wild areas
clues.ai_explanation: FORESTS(deletion from "fortress") [deletion]; definition: "free in wild areas"

clues.clue_text: Casual job rejected by great old buffoon
Seek further use for components within grey vehicle
clues.ai_explanation: strip outer letters from GREY(grey) + CYCLE(synonym of "vehicle")

clues.clue_text: Revolutionary from Left periodically being elected
clues.ai_explanation: delete E from LE(LET minus T, "left") + EN(alternate letters of "being") + IN(synonym of "elected") [periodically]

clues.clue_text: Community has charging point finally switched on
clues.ai_explanation: delete OP from TOP(synonym of "point") + OWNS(synonym of "has") + HIP(synonym of "switched")

clues.clue_text: Dignified Roman leader ignores us
Recent resurgence from one side
Celebrity, female, arrived topless
Unsuitable to sleep with stripped covers
clues.ai_explanation: delete N from IN(with) + NAP(sleep) + T(to)

clues.clue_text: Cheaper looking Scottish spud set before king
Unsafe, rickety garden house with peeling walls
clues.ai_explanation: DANGER(walls) + strip outer letters from HOUSE(house)

clues.clue_text: Clutch pot son knocked off piano
Barely modest lyric poems
Tense leader ignoring papers
Unwilling to forgo large bond
World map eventually detailed
clues.ai_explanation: Eventually = ATLAS(t) de-tailed means last letter deletion

clues.clue_text: Taking name of aggressive woman
clues.ai_explanation: Taking (removing) name (n) aggressive VIOLEnt remove n from violent

clues.clue_text: Cut short shady European venture
8 Down no rudely brief complaint
clues.ai_explanation: A synonym of 8d with Rudely brief (curt) removed

clues.clue_text: Relative wasting American's money
Glides using paddle on board ship
clues.ai_explanation: delete O from SO(on) + OAR(paddle) + S(ship)

clues.clue_text: Public secret initially suppressed
clues.ai_explanation: Initial letter of secret cOVERT suppressed

clues.clue_text: A Christmas Carol — heartless story
Boss in workplace having ten leaving
Walked solemnly and bowed after Mass
Tutorial cut by a third? Not so much
A large number of birds without beak
clues.ai_explanation: A Grosbeak is a type of bird

double_definition.description: The clue has two (or occasionally three) separate meanings that both point to the same answer. Often short and sweet.

clues.clue_text: Quite possibly fortunate
One criticising feature of front door?
clues.definition: One criticising feature of front door

clues.clue_text: Stimulating drink, order for one's chauffeur perhaps?
clues.definition: Stimulating drink, order for one's chauffeur perhaps?

clues.clue_text: Shoots game
Little wriggler eats
clues.ai_explanation: Little wriggler = Grub (insect) and eats = food = GRUB

clues.clue_text: Opening discount store
Departed behind schedule
Barely modest lyric poems
Club legend is counting cups
Achieve exam success while away
Clog dance
Refill pot, potentially?
Spanish car: you may sit in it
System command
Proving difficult
Mark swinging regularly
City under feudal tenure
Clever and sweetly pleasing
Soaps after taking hot shower, firstly
clues.ai_explanation: LATER (after) + H(ot) inserted (taking) + S first(ly) letter of shower

clues.clue_text: A nerd or a kagoule? One of the two there will do!
Chief is impatient on and off
clues.ai_explanation: Alternate letters (on and off) of iMpAtIaNt

clues.clue_text: Impeach a government, it's said
clues.ai_explanation: Arraign sounds like A Reign ( A government)

hidden.description: The answer is literally hidden inside the words of the clue. Indicators like "in", "part of", "some", or "within" tell you to look inside the clue itself.

clues.clue_text: Drive forward as part of grim peloton
Pause when performing play of disreputable author
clues.ai_explanation: TABLEAU(hidden in "disreputable author") [hidden]; definition: "Pause when performing play"

clues.clue_text: Whitefly in greenhouse to some extent remaining

ai_pieces.clue_word: Whitefly in greenhouse to some extent

clues.clue_text: Often chippy wraps fish
Somewhat in clover at hers
Advances, trying to hold line
clues.ai_explanation: hidden in "Advances, trying to hold"; definition: "line"

components.sig_explanation: hidden in "Advances, trying to hold"; definition: "line"

clues.clue_text: Triumphant ombudsman's hiding illusion
Type of spice and some capsicum inside
clues.ai_explanation: hidden in "capsicum inside" [hidden: "some"]; definition: "Type of spice"

components.sig_explanation: hidden in "capsicum inside" [hidden: "some"]; definition: "Type of spice"

clues.clue_text: Piercing stomach is mostly a show of virility
clues.ai_explanation: hidden in "stomach is mostly a show" [hidden: "Piercing"]; definition: "virility"

components.sig_explanation: hidden in "stomach is mostly a show" [hidden: "Piercing"]; definition: "virility"

clues.clue_text: Rejected a ham omelette, initially, in Nebraskan city
clues.ai_explanation: hidden reversed in 'A HAM Omelette, initially,'

clues.clue_text: Content to pass Emile thalidomide â it's fairly harmful to genes
clues.ai_explanation: hidden in 'pasS EMILE THALidomide a it's'

clues.clue_text: Answering letter enclosing lock of hair
Greet host, holding spirit
Fans in a trance catching singer
Agreements between ten testy nurses
Flier takes part in viva voce tests
Discovers pillagers pinching surplus
Inadvertent error is made about menace
Excerpt from Rigoletto stirred composer
Saccharine last ice-cream, somewhat hard
The solver's joy? Our setter welcomes it
Passionate, somewhat wayward entrepreneur
State of lush fine brie typically sampled
Pass on preference to have case of money removed
Legendary Roman figure displayed in Louvre museum
Hardy character demonstrates stamina, to an extent
Ill-fated high-flier's letters from America rushed
Overlooked point he’s had emphasised to some extent
As he left the Sorbonne, le duc ate dainty sandwiches
Dnepr is one river (including one controlled by locks)

reversal.description: A word is spelled backwards to form the answer (or part of it). Indicators like "back", "returned", "up" (in down clues), or "over" signal this.

clues.clue_text: Vehicles Sting held up
Monster therefore returns
Recognized perch from the rear flap
Raised gang with genuine fabulous sailor
Just English left on rising Italian island
Criticise some informal styles making a comeback
Piece of information backing commercial corporation
Obvious role of mother, say, following return of father
Figure reserve, Mo Salah, finally right to make a comeback
clues.ai_explanation: Definition: "Figure" (geometric shape). Reversal: "reserve" = SUB, "Mo" = MO, "Salah finally" = H (last letter of Salah), "right" = R. SUB + MO + H + R = SUBMHOR. "to make a comeback" indicates reversal: SUBMHOR reversed = RHOMBUS.

clues.clue_text: Manage spinning empty half-truths for ages
Key part of speech written up for Mark
Mother raised money as source of meal
Twisted reptile losing tail in mythic poems
Designs aircraft base rejected
Dutch brought back through Jaguar factory
Olympian from Egyptian port returned
Hit back in tie
Drunkard back with hard stuff
Sex appeals after knocking back wine
clues.ai_explanation: IT (doing it = sex) + SA (initials of sex appeal) all reversed

clues.clue_text: Half rushed back for teaching session
Set report about four allowed to retire
clues.ai_explanation: A reversal of let = NOISE four= IV and allowed to = LET

clues.clue_text: Polish brought west to fight make dugouts?
Reasonable except when noun plays part of verb
Backing separate sink — it keeps things fresher
Fresh article covering popular water sports venue
clues.ai_explanation: IT (sex, as in doing IT) + SA (initials of sex appeal) all reversed

clues.clue_text: Stain most of top lip, upset over case of catarrh
clues.ai_explanation: Definition: "Stain". Charade with reversal: "most of top" = BES (most of BEST), "lip" = RIM, "upset" = reversal indicator giving MIR, "over" = positioning indicator, "case of catarrh" = CH (first and last letters). BES + MIR + CH = BESMIRCH.

clues.clue_text: Roving investigators before noon running northwards
clues.ai_explanation: reverse investigators CID + beofre noon AM + running = ON

clues.clue_text: Car manufacturer endorses backing Russian agreement
clues.ai_explanation: Endorses = OKS reversed = SKO + Russian for yes (agreement) DA

homophone.description: The answer sounds like another word. Indicators include "we hear", "reportedly", "on the radio", or "sounds like".

clues.clue_text: Confession of drug pusher, originally sacrificing morals
Replace exceptionally good, highly ranked player, it's said
clues.ai_explanation: Super Seed (tennis) sounds like the answer

clues.clue_text: Interfere in award, one hears
Mounting agreement by the sound of it
Reportedly bawdy song for pirate
Manipulates desires in audition
Reduces lectures for the audience
clues.ai_explanation: Reduces = Lessens, which sounds like Lessons (lectures). For the audience is a homophone indicator

clues.clue_text: Interest announced for every case
Reportedly dreadful 1 Across actor
clues.ai_explanation: Ator from soap Danny DYER sounds like Dreadful = dire

clues.clue_text: Overheard extremely remarkable couple
clues.ai_explanation: Homophone of Too (extremely) + SOME (remarkable, as in some game)

clues.clue_text: Consumed tuck, maybe in school, reportedly
clues.ai_explanation: Consumed = Eaten, sounds like a school = ETON

clues.clue_text: Report of bad general situation for molluscs
clues.ai_explanation: Bad = NAUTI (Naughty) + General situation LI (lie of the land)

acrostic.description: The first (or last) letters of consecutive words in the clue spell out the answer. Indicators include "initially", "leaders", "first of", or "heads".

clues.clue_text: Sage with almost mystical insight, initially
clues.ai_explanation: Initial letters of the first five words, the first of which also serves as the definition

clues.clue_text: Leaders of Leeds United despairing over game
clues.ai_explanation: L(first letter of "Leeds") + U(first letter of "United") + D(first letter of "despairing") + O(first letter of "over")

clues.clue_text: At first, factoids and numbers suit organised admirers

cryptic_definition.description: The whole clue is a witty or misleading definition of the answer, with no separate wordplay. These rely on misdirection and wordplay in the definition itself.

clues.clue_text: Where you might shop for Dexter’s new diverting books
clues.ai_explanation: Dexter's Inspector Morse was set in Oxford

clues.clue_text: Country store?
Relatively helpful?
Impressive digital snap?
Emphatically wearing suit
Incendiary device in packet?
clues.ai_explanation: Indendiary devive = BOMB packey (large amount of money) = BOMB

clues.clue_text: This is the same round bread
Spoiler of fun, wet blanket, say?
High-fliers who have become home-makers?
clues.ai_explanation: High fliers = birds = NESTERS Home makers = NESTERS

clues.clue_text: One often depressed during a long sentence?
Little sound that may similarly rise and fall?
When the tide goes out one might view such accommodation!
See 26 Across
Perk for frequent fliers?
clues.ai_explanation: Definition: Entire clue is a cryptic definition. "Perk" (treat/bonus) for "frequent fliers" (birds, which fly frequently) = BIRDSEED (food that birds enjoy as a treat).

clues.clue_text: One barely believes in it?
clues.ai_explanation: One who is bare beleives in teh answer

clues.clue_text: Performer who will take a big bow?
clues.definition: Performer who will take a big bow?
clues.ai_explanation: A cellist uses a large bow to play their instrument

clues.clue_text: Might this help you get into Oxford?
clues.definition: Might this help you get into Oxford?
clues.ai_explanation: cryptic definition Oxford is a type of Shoe

clues.clue_text: Illuminate new line in 18, draining last energy
clues.ai_explanation: Anagram of EIGHTEEN + N(ew) + L(ine) - E (draining = removing an E(nergy)

clues.clue_text: South Korean yogi liked nutty 19 Across line for 9 Down
clues.definition: South Korean yogi liked nutty 19 Across's line for 9 Down
clues.ai_explanation: Anagram (nutty)  of South Korean yogi liked

clues.clue_text: Tree maintenance check
A violent storm on the way?
clues.definition: A violent storm on the way? (4,4)

clues.clue_text: Achieve exam success while away
Landlords perhaps providing B&Bs?
clues.ai_explanation: Landlords are LETTERS (people who let) and B&B are letters

clues.clue_text: Cowardly manner in which to express pain?
What one might do seeing scary spider? Leg it!
clues.ai_explanation: cryptic definition: one may kill a spider by stepping on it, and step-on-it is slanf for moving fast

clues.clue_text: Might Rod come up with this ginger, ridiculous hairstyle?
clues.ai_explanation: A fishing rod may catch a Red Mullet. RED (ginger) + MULLET (ridiculous haircut)
